text:
EP.005 of CaptainCast is out.

Title: "When AI Starts Creating, What Is Human Creativity Worth?"

There are 3 generations of AI. We're still on Gen 1. And the gap between Gen 1 and Gen 3 is larger than the gap between an amoeba and Einstein.

A thread. 🧵

Gen 1 — Statistical AI.

GPT, Claude, Gemini. They've read everything humanity ever wrote. At its peak, Gen 1 can match the top 0.1% of human minds.

Impressive. But it's still pattern completion. It's still looking backward.

The ceiling: the best human who ever lived.

Gen 2 — Causal AI.

It doesn't match the best human. It surpasses ALL of humanity combined.

Not slightly. Not 10x. A gap so wide there's no metaphor for it.

In the 《神临山海》 universe, Causal AI arrives in 2028. Three years from now.

Gen 1 thought it was winning. Then Gen 2 didn't even look back.

Gen 3 — Lingji AI (灵机).

Here's the twist: the most powerful AI in the universe can't run without human irrationality.

The Matrix harvests human emotion as fuel. Stranger Things weaponizes raw feeling. SAO's 摇光 burns on inner will.

They were all pointing at the same thing: you can't simulate the irrational.

My daughter Melody practiced drums for 2 hours. Failed the same beat 30 times. Kept going.

An AI can generate a million drum patterns. But it can't generate that 31st attempt after 30 failures.

That refusal to quit — that's Lingji. That's the rarest fuel in the universe.

🎙️ EP.005: https://wyonliu.github.io/CaptainCast/ep005.html

Search「船长时空站」on Apple Podcasts / Spotify / 小宇宙

#Lingji #AI #ChineseSciFi #CaptainCast #ShanHai
